context: Attribute names with labels, discrete levels, variable role (risk factor / disease / symptom / test / derived), and brief clinical description — functional context without explicit causal direction.

variables.name: dysp
variables.label: Dyspnoea
variables.description: Shortness of breath reported by the patient; a respiratory symptom that can arise from multiple pulmonary or airway conditions.

variables.name: tub
variables.label: Tuberculosis
variables.description: Pulmonary tuberculosis caused by Mycobacterium tuberculosis; a chronic infectious disease affecting the lungs.

variables.name: lung
variables.label: Lung cancer
variables.description: Primary malignancy of the lung; associated with long-term exposure to carcinogens including tobacco smoke.

variables.name: bronc
variables.label: Bronchitis
variables.description: Inflammation of the bronchial tubes causing cough and airflow obstruction; strongly associated with smoking.

variables.name: asia
variables.label: Visit to Asia
variables.description: Recent travel to Asia; a geographic exposure variable that elevates the prior probability of tuberculosis due to higher regional prevalence.

variables.name: smoke
variables.label: Smoking
variables.description: Current or recent tobacco smoking status; a primary risk factor for both lung cancer and bronchitis.

variables.name: xray
variables.label: Chest X-ray abnormal
variables.description: Result of a chest radiograph; abnormal (positive) findings can indicate tuberculosis, lung cancer, or other pulmonary pathology but lack specificity.

variables.name: either
variables.label: Tuberculosis or lung cancer present
variables.description: Binary intermediate node indicating the logical disjunction of tuberculosis and lung cancer; used to route their combined influence on downstream variables.
